# Wicklee

> Sovereign GPU fleet monitor for local AI inference.
> One Rust binary per node, React dashboard at localhost:7700, fleet aggregation at wicklee.dev.
> WES (Wicklee Efficiency Score) = tok/s / (Watts x ThermalPenalty) ‚Äî the MPG for local AI.

## Docs

- [Full Documentation](https://wicklee.dev/llms-full.txt)
- [Documentation (Markdown)](https://wicklee.dev/docs.md)
- [API Reference (Markdown)](https://wicklee.dev/api.md)
- [OpenAPI Spec](https://wicklee.dev/openapi.json)
- [Metrics Reference](https://wicklee.dev/metrics.md)
- [Interactive Docs](https://wicklee.dev/docs)

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://wicklee.dev/install.sh | bash
```

Windows: `irm https://wicklee.dev/install.ps1 | iex`

## Localhost API (no auth, any tier)

- `GET /api/metrics` ‚Äî SSE stream, 1 Hz telemetry
- `GET /ws` ‚Äî WebSocket, 10 Hz telemetry
- `GET /api/observations` ‚Äî Local hardware pattern evaluation (4 patterns)
- `GET /api/history?node_id=WK-XXXX` ‚Äî DuckDB metric history (1h buffer)
- `GET /api/traces` ‚Äî Proxy inference traces
- `GET /api/events/history` ‚Äî Node event log
- `GET /api/events/recent` ‚Äî Recent in-memory events
- `GET /api/export?format=json|csv` ‚Äî Data export
- `GET /api/tags` ‚Äî Ollama model tags
- `GET /api/pair/status` ‚Äî Pairing status
- `POST /mcp` ‚Äî MCP (Model Context Protocol) JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint
- `GET /.well-known/mcp.json` ‚Äî MCP server manifest

### MCP Tools (via POST /mcp)

- `get_node_status` ‚Äî Full hardware + inference metrics snapshot
- `get_inference_state` ‚Äî Live/idle/busy state with sensor context
- `get_active_models` ‚Äî Running models across Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp
- `get_observations` ‚Äî Local hardware pattern evaluation
- `get_metrics_history` ‚Äî 1-hour rolling telemetry buffer

### MCP Resources

- `wicklee://node/metrics` ‚Äî Live MetricsPayload JSON
- `wicklee://node/thermal` ‚Äî Thermal state + WES penalty values

## Fleet API v1 (X-API-Key auth)

Base URL: `https://wicklee.dev/api/v1`

- `GET /api/v1/fleet` ‚Äî All nodes with full MetricsPayload
- `GET /api/v1/fleet/wes` ‚Äî WES scores ranked
- `GET /api/v1/nodes/{id}` ‚Äî Single node deep dive
- `GET /api/v1/route/best` ‚Äî Routing recommendation (latency or efficiency)
- `GET /api/v1/insights/latest` ‚Äî Fleet intelligence snapshot (Team+)
- `POST /api/v1/keys` ‚Äî Create API key
- `GET /api/v1/keys` ‚Äî List API keys
- `DELETE /api/v1/keys/{id}` ‚Äî Revoke API key
- `GET /metrics` ‚Äî Prometheus scrape endpoint (Team+, X-API-Key auth)

## Key Metrics

- `inference_state`: "live" | "idle-spd" | "busy" | "idle"
- `ollama_tokens_per_second`: tok/s from 20-token probe (~30s cadence)
- `apple_soc_power_w`: Combined CPU+GPU+ANE power (Apple Silicon)
- `nvidia_power_draw_w`: Board power (NVIDIA)
- `thermal_state`: "Normal" | "Fair" | "Serious" | "Critical"
- `penalty_avg`: Thermal penalty multiplier (1.0 = no penalty)
- `vllm_requests_waiting`: Queue depth (vLLM)
- `ollama_ttft_ms`: Time to first token (Ollama probe baseline)
- `vllm_avg_ttft_ms`: Time to first token (vLLM production histogram)

## 18 Observation Patterns (A-R)

Community (9): A (Thermal Drain), B (Phantom Load), C (WES Velocity Drop),
F (Memory Trajectory), H (Power Jitter), J (Swap Pressure), K (Clock Drift),
N (NVIDIA Thermal Ceiling), O (VRAM Overcommit)

Pro (9): D (Power-GPU Decoupling), E (Fleet Load Imbalance), G (Bandwidth Saturation),
I (Efficiency Penalty Drag), L (PCIe Degradation), M (vLLM KV Cache Saturation),
P (TTFT Regression), Q (Latency Spike), R (vLLM Queue Saturation)

## Runtimes Supported

- Ollama (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- vLLM (Linux)
- llama.cpp / llama-box (macOS, Linux)

## Pricing

- Community: Free forever, 3 nodes, 24h history, 9 patterns
- Pro: $9/mo, 10 nodes, 7-day history, 18 patterns, Slack alerts
- Team: $19/seat/mo (3-seat min), 25 nodes, 90-day history, shared dashboards
- Enterprise: From $200/mo, proxy, airgapped/sovereign deployment
